Quelle: The Guardian / Council on Foreign Relations / World Economic Forum
Zusammenfassung: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are scheduled to meet in Beijing on May 14-15, 2026. The summit aims to stabilize relations strained by disputes over trade, Taiwan, and the ongoing war in Iran. Economic deliverables are expected to be prioritized, with potential for an extension of the trade truce and significant Chinese purchases of US goods. China's trade grew faster than expected in April, reversing a decline in exports to the United States, although year-to-date bilateral trade has decreased. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is invited to join the US delegation.
